title_en: Kathynmarissa Leads Trifecta Sweep for Brown in Diana
description: Trainer Chad Brown continues his dominance of the Diana Stakes (G1T) at Saratoga Race Course, winning the prestigious turf stakes with Kathynmarissa—a record-extending 11th victory in the race for her trainer.

title_en: Knightsbridge Dominates Two-Turn Test in Monmouth Cup
description: The Bill Mott-trained Knightsbridge answered the two-turn question with a dominant victory in the July 18 Monmouth Cup (G3) at Monmouth Park. The Godolphin homebred cruised over the previously undefeated Money Game, with Skippylongstocking third.

title_en: Johanna Walsh Streaks Clear to Win Irish Oaks
description: Johanna Walsh proved an able deputy for Joseph O'Brien by landing the Irish Oaks (G1) July 18 at the Curragh. O'Brien was forced to scratch Epsom Oaks (G1) winner and favorite Thundering On because she was reportedly off her feed.

title_en: Proletariat First Stakes Winner for Raging Bull
description: Proletariat becomes the first stakes winner for second-crop sire Raging Bull in notching a front-running triumph in the $153,000 Oceanside Stakes at Del Mar on the track's opening day of its summer meet, July 17.

title_en: Stakes Preview: Haskell and Diana
description: Megan Burgess takes a look at the Haskell Stakes at Monmouth Park & the Diana Stakes at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: War Front Pensioned Following 2026 Breeding Season
description: Claiborne Farm's prominent and internationally successful sire War Front, a son of Danzig, sired 121 black-type winners worldwide, including 26 grade/group 1 winners. His progeny earned more than $113 million.

title_en: Sadler Trio Poised to Take Flight During Del Mar Meet
description: In this week's On Racing, trainer John Sadler is looking forward to watching the squadron of 2-year-old Flightlines poised to take flight this year, including a trio from his own barn.

title_en: Jersey-Bred Book'em Danno Returns to Monmouth a Star
description: Move over, Bruce Springsteen. New Jersey has a new rock star. And he has four legs!

title_en: Woodbine Announces TV, Streaming Platforms for 2026
description: Woodbine announces a new partnership with Citytv, Sportsnet, and CBC that will bring its marquee racing events to audiences across network television and streaming platforms throughout the 2026 racing season.

title_en: Injury Shelves Reef Runner for Rest of 2026
description: The grade 1 winner and 5-year-old gelding by The Big Beast is expected to return to racing next year, according to trainer David Fawkes. Reef Runner has career earnings of nearly $2.2 million.

title_en: Questions About Hawthorne Shutdown Are Left to Court
description: With no savior in sight for Hawthorne Race Course, the Illinois Racing Board has canceled its July 22 meeting; next steps in track shutdown are up to the bankruptcy judge.

title_en: Trainer Bernie Flint, 'A True Horseman,' Dies at 86
description: Bernie Flint, a grade 1-winning conditioner with 3,551 victories in Thoroughbred races, died early July 17 at a Louisville, Ky., hospital following a battle with bladder cancer, according to his son, Steve. He was 86.

title_en: Goodman Named Thoroughbred Club of America Honor Guest
description: Greg Goodman has been selected by the board of directors of the Thoroughbred Club of America as the 2026 Honor Guest, club president W. Chapman Hopkins announced July 17.

title_en: Book Value Upgrades to Mandaloun's First Stakes Winner
description: The 2-year-old gelding is now 2-for-2 for owner Marion Gorham. Book Value was bred and is trained by Robert Gorham, Marion's brother.

title_en: Romans Has Bobrovsky Ready for Return in Quick Call
description: Trainer Dale Romans, who co-owns Bobrovsky with Steve Berg, discusses the 3-year-old son of Daredevil before he returns from a nine-month layoff in the $225,000 Quick Call Stakes (G3T) at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Handicapping a Competitive Renewal of the Haskell
description: J. Keeler Johnson handicaps the 2026 Haskell (G1) at Monmouth Park.

title_en: Prospective Pinhooks: Fasig-Tipton The Saratoga Sale
description: Sale histories for horses entered in Fasig-Tipton's The Saratoga Sale, its select yearling sale in Saratoga Springs, N.Y.

title_en: BloodHorse MarketWatch: 2026 Juvenile Sales Review
description: A statistical review of the 2-year-olds in training sales season for 2026.

title_en: Golden Tempo Resumes Breezing at Keeneland
description: Keeneland-based dual classic winner Golden Tempo works 4 furlongs in :48 2/5 July 17 in preparation for his next scheduled start, the Aug. 29 Travers Stakes (G1) at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Hong Kong Jockey Club Reports Increased Handle
description: Total racing wagering turnover for the Hong Kong 2025-26 season reached HK$143.31 billion (US$18.28 billion), representing a 3.2% increase on the 2024-25 season. This marks an encouraging return to growth, the Hong Kong Jockey Club said.

title_en: Poor Air Quality Causes Numerous Racing Cancellations
description: Racing July 17 at Woodbine, Colonial Downs, Delaware Park, and Charles Town has been canceled due to poor air quality.

title_en: Godolphin's International Star Rebel's Romance Retired
description: Rebel's Romance, one of Godolphin's most prolific superstars with nine top-level successes around the world, has been retired following his victory in the July 9 Princess of Wales's Stakes (G2) at Newmarket.

title_en: Selling 'Solo' Was Not an Easy Decision for Gold
description: Receiving what was described by one person involved in the sale as "an offer he could not refuse," Al Gold suppressed his heavy heart and sold Preakness Stakes (G1) winner Napoleon Solo to Espoir USA for an undisclosed, but no doubt staggering, sum.

title_en: De La Rose Stakes: And One More Time
description: De La Rose Stakes

title_en: Vivacious Stakes: Awesomesauce
description: Vivacious Stakes

title_en: Luster Poised to Keep Sparkling in Woodbine Oaks
description: Luster, coming off her first graded stakes victory in the June 27 Selene Stakes (G3) at Woodbine, figures to shine when she battles six other Canadian-bred 3-year-old fillies in the July 19 Woodbine Oaks.

title_en: Johnny's Red Storm, Ewing Among Returnees in Quick Call
description: An overflow field is entered July 19 for the $225,000 Quick Call Stakes (G3T) at Saratoga Race Course, and all eyes will be on those returning from layoffs, such as Johnny's Red Storm, Ewing, and Bobrovsky.

title_en: Bentornato Sidelined With Sesamoid Injury
description: Trainer Jose D'Angelo confirmed with BloodHorse that the ridgling is expected to race in 2027.

title_en: From the Magazine: Coming Up Green at Del Mar
description: Grass racing rules a powerful portion of the Del Mar product, which will be on display July 17 through Labor Day, Sept. 7. Of the 21 graded stakes scheduled over the 32-day summer meet, 10 have been earmarked for the turf.

title_en: Splendora Brings Elite Credentials Into Molly Pitcher
description: 2025 Breeders' Cup Filly and Mare Sprint (G1) winner Splendora, victorious in six of her last seven starts, seeks another graded stakes victory in the $500,000 Molly Pitcher Stakes (G2) July 18 at Monmouth Park.

title_en: Globe-Trotting Group 1 Winner Khaadem Retired at Age 10
description: Trainer Charlie Hills described Khaadem as a "horse of a lifetime" after announcing the retirement of his dual group 1-winning star following his run at Royal Ascot last month.

title_en: Bolger Seeks Third Irish Oaks With Sparan Nua
description: Jim Bolger returns to the group 1 spotlight in a bid to win his third Irish Oaks (G1) with Sparan Nua featured in a top-class final field of 11 for the fillies' classic July 18 at the Curragh.

title_en: Woodbine Cancels July 16 Card Over Poor Air Quality
description: While working with Horsemen's Benevolent and Protective Association and the Alcohol and Gaming Commission of Ontario, Woodbine Racetrack has canceled its live racing card July 16 due to poor air quality.

title_en: Kornacki Among Racing and Gaming Conference Speakers
description: The Racing and Gaming Conference at Saratoga announced July 16 an exceptional lineup of speakers for its 2026 conference Aug. 10-12 at the Hilton in Saratoga Springs, N.Y.

title_en: Surrounding Development Could Boost Monmouth's Future
description: In this month's At the Track with Sean Collins, Monmouth Park's deal to receive 50% of the revenue from surrounding development projects will hopefully lead to an improving racing product in the future.

title_en: Champion Sprinter Book'em Danno Returns Home to NJ
description: Atlantic Six Racing's Book'em Danno will be the star attraction on the titular "Book'em Danno Day" at Monmouth Park, as the 2025 champion male sprinter headlines a likely field of seven in the $200,000 Mr. Prospector Stakes July 17.

title_en: San Diego Showdown Pits Journalism Against Full Serrano
description: The $300,000 San Diego Handicap (G2) July 18 at Del Mar will provide Journalism and Full Serrano a chance to get back in the winning groove.

title_en: Stonerside Co-Founder Janice McNair Dies at 89
description: Janice McNair, who founded Stonerside Stable with her husband Bob in 1984 before leaving the industry to launch the National Football League's Houston Texans, died July 14. She was 89.

title_en: Retired Ghostzapper Continues to Impress as a Sire
description: In this week's Porter on Pedigrees, Ghostzapper was represented during the July 11-12 weekend by similarly bred graded stakes winner Ole Crazy Bone and stakes winner Ghost Me.

title_en: CTHS Ontario Details Enhanced Breeders' Incentives
description: The Canadian Thoroughbred Horse Society announces details of Ontario's Breeders' Awards and breeder incentive programs, made possible through the CA$5 million increase to Ontario Racing's Thoroughbred Improvement Program.

title_en: Girvin Filly at $450,000 Leads McCrocklin's $1.46M Haul
description: Pinhooker Tom McCrocklin emerges as the leading yearling buyer July 14 during The July Sale at Fasig-Tipton in Lexington, purchasing eight yearlings, led by Hip 166, a daughter of Girvin who brought $450,000 to top the filly market.

title_en: Angel of Kirk, Talleyrand Deliver on Promise
description: Angel of Kirk and Talleyrand, two smartly bred juveniles, are new BloodHorse Maiden Watch Hot Prospects after posting first-out victories.

title_en: Sports Executive Maffei Keynote Speaker at Round Table
description: The Jockey Club today announced the agenda, including a list of speakers on timely topics, for its 2026 Round Table Conference on Matters Pertaining to Racing.

title_en: Four Star Sales Welcomes Hamilton to its Team
description: Four Star Sales has announced that Grace Hamilton has joined the company as bloodstock and sales coordinator. Hamilton joins Four Star following graduation from the internationally respected Godolphin Flying Start program.

title_en: Crazy Hot Mess Becomes First U.S. Winner for Pinehurst
description: Crazy Hot Mess became the first winner in the U.S. for Walmac Farm's Pinehurst when the 2-year-old filly broke her maiden on debut at Ellis Park to win a maiden special weight race by 1 1/4 lengths.

title_en: Liam's Law Becomes First Winner for Colonel Liam
description: Liam's Law became the first winner for Ocala Stud's multiple graded stakes winner and freshman sire Colonel Liam, after breaking his maiden July 12 in a maiden special weight race over the turf at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Taylor Made Offers Half Sister to Booked
description: First-crop sire Two Phil's will be represented by Hip 52, a half sister to recent Sanford Stakes (G3) winner Booked. The filly sells July 14 at Fasig-Tipton's The July Sale with potential to become her dam's third stakes performer.

title_en: Rattle N Roll Retired, Heads to Stud in Saudi Arabia
description: Grade 1 winner Rattle N Roll, an earner of nearly $4 million, has been retired from racing at age 7 because of bone bruising, and will begin a stallion career in Saudi Arabia in 2027, according to co-owner Mike Mackin of Lucky Seven Stable.

title_en: Classic Winners Headline July Sale's First-Crop Sires
description: Kentucky Derby (G1) winner Mage and Belmont Stakes (G1) winner Arcangelo are among 19 first-crop yearling sires represented in Fasig-Tipton's The July Sale being held July 14 in Lexington.

title_en: Juddmonte Stallion Zamindar Dies at 32
description: Smart racehorse and decorated stallion Zamindar has died at the age of 32, Juddmonte reported July 10.

title_en: MD Horse Breeders Association Board Selects Officers
description: The board of directors of the Maryland Horse Breeders Association reelects Richard Blue as president, Josh Pons as vice president, and Michael Horning as secretary/treasurer.

title_en: Ontario Thoroughbred Industry Gets $10M Boost
description: Ontario Racing announces that $10 million in new funding for the province's Thoroughbred racing industry will be used to invest in increased breeders' rewards, purses, and overall expansion of Ontario-bred racing opportunities.

title_en: Velvet Beretta Becomes Cyberknife's First Winner
description: The first-out winner captured a 5 1/2-furlong maiden special weight July 9 at Ellis Park by 3 lengths.

title_en: Country Life Farm to Stand Regally Bred Provenance
description: While injury limited on-track opportunities for grade 2-placed Provenance, the son of seven-time leading sire Into Mischief out of two-time champion Monomoy Girl will bring plenty of star power to Maryland when he stands at Country Life Farm in 2027.

title_en: When Sweet Azteca Runs, She's Very, Very Good
description: The daughter of Sharp Azteca has started 10 times in three years, but owns six graded stakes wins among those starts. The key to her female family's success could be in the daughters' mitochondrial DNA.

title_en: Broodmare Retirement Summit July 24 at Keeneland
description: Mareworthy Charities will host the Thoroughbred Broodmare Retirement Summit from 1-3 p.m. Friday, July 24, at Keeneland. The event will update the work to strengthen long-term outcomes for mares after their breeding careers end.

title_en: Scarlett Begonia, Antivenom Blossom at Ellis Park
description: Two nicely bred juveniles became BloodHorse Maiden Watch Hot Prospects with comfortable debut victories at Ellis Park. Antivenom, a son of Candy Ride, broke his maiden July 4 before the Life Is Good filly Scarlett Begonia followed suit July 6.

description: 2.40 Curragh, Mdn, 2yo, f, 7fT SPECKLES (IRE) ( No Nay Never ) is one of three from Ballydoyle in this maiden won six years ago by the high-class Snowfall and not the pick of Ryan Moore, but as a daughter of the Irish and Yorkshire Oaks heroine Seventh Heaven (Galileo) warrants close attention.
